sourceMovie.title: Coherence
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Coherence (2014) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. James Ward Byrkit's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Predestination
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Coherence clicked for you, Predestination (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Coherence?
faq.answer: Try Predestination, The Island, Pitch Black—each shares DNA with Coherence while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Coherence?
faq.answer: Coherence (2014) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Coherence part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Coherence Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
